summary: Relocates the agent-facing docs tree from the repo-root docs/ to .materia/docs/ (breaking; artifact schema 3 → 4, with a docs-location doctor check and a relocate-docs auto-migration); restructures /materia:init's intake into a staged eight-chapter interview (AskUserQuestion-driven, every non-essential question skippable) and grows the scaffold product brief it materializes: § Goals & success, a tagline slot, Brand & color slots, and the 'Not yet decided' honest-gap convention; lands five stage-review angles (design-coherence, design-feasibility, design-fidelity, architecture-grounding, architecture-coverage) under two new Gate tokens (design-stage, architecture-stage), growing the canonical review-angle count from seven to twelve and wiring the pre-human-gate design-stage review (design.md plus the design's visual half and committed snapshot) and the pre-plan-tasks architecture-stage review into ship-spec (and, over bug-analysis.md, into fix-bug); generalizes the autopilot-only post-finalize merge watch into a shared PR-watch phase that runs on every ship-spec/fix-bug run — an interactive run surfaces the PR at green for review while an --auto run additionally merges; retires ui-inspection in favor of two live-app maintainers, curator (visuals) and concierge (experience), landing the shared Maintainer lifecycle contract janitor and librarian are retrofitted onto; refreshes the Materia sigil's closing clause and operator-facing announcement flavor; and corrects two scaffold templates (the retro touchpoint enumeration; the bug STATUS template's ui-coverage-waiver convention).

changes.summary: The scaffold product brief (.materia/docs/product.md) gains a § Goals & success section, a tagline slot, explicit Brand & color slots, and a 'Not yet decided' skip convention; /materia:init's intake becomes a staged eight-chapter interview that fills them.
changes.detectionNotes: .materia/docs/product.md is materialized once at init and thereafter user-owned prose the repo freely edits; the absence of the new sections/slots in an installed brief is indistinguishable from a deliberate local edit, so no doctor check can honestly flag it. The interview behavior itself lives in the plugin skill and updates with the plugin.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized docs to match the current scaffold if desired: in .materia/docs/product.md add § Goals & success (why it exists / success metrics / milestones / business model), the Tagline slot under § Name & positioning, and the Brand & color bullet under § Design feel & taste — filling each from your own answers, or with an explicit 'Not yet decided — <what was asked>.' line; then extend the brief-content enumerations in CLAUDE.md, .materia/docs/README.md, and .materia/docs/standards/docs.md with goals. The staged-interview behavior lives in the plugin skill and updates with the plugin — no repo action needed for that part.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). No artifactSchema bump — a content change to a materialized, user-owned doc; no canonical-location or installed-artifact-set move. Classified optional (operator decision at intake): nothing in this plugin version consumes the new brief sections yet, so an installed repo loses no capability by keeping its older brief — the design/propose-spec grounding reads sections that already existed. Also folded under this entry: survey→interview rewording of init comments in the scaffold's MATERIA.md, glossary.md, and surface-map.md (comments deleted at materialization time).

changes.summary: Lands five stage-review angles (design-coherence, design-feasibility, design-fidelity, architecture-grounding, architecture-coverage) in the review-angle library and MATERIA.md § Review angles registry under two new Gate tokens, design-stage and architecture-stage (canonical count seven → twelve); ship-spec spawns the design-stage angles after design.md is authored, before the human design gate's first arrival — design-fidelity also reviewing the design's visual half and committed snapshot through a capability-gated visibility ladder (direct canvas read, orchestrator-inlined read-back, or snapshot alone; skipped-and-recorded on a repo-side design with no visual half), with the first snapshot export pinned to precede the review — and the architecture-stage angles after architecture.md returns ok, before plan-tasks, while fix-bug spawns the architecture-stage angles analogously over bug-analysis.md — each point an orchestrator-spawned, ≤3-round adversarial loop (ship-spec/SKILL.md § Stage reviews (design & architecture)) over the registry's default per-angle tier, which the orchestrator may override per run.
changes.detectionNotes: The review-angle library (.materia/review-angles/) is a forkable, hand-reconciled artifact the user repo freely edits — doctor's inspect() never scans it, and a missing angle file or registry row is not mechanically distinguishable from a deliberate local removal. ship-spec/SKILL.md § Stage reviews (design & architecture)'s zero-rows path already handles this at runtime, not a doctor check: zero registered rows for a point skip that review point and record `stage-review(<point>): skipped (no <token> angles registered)` in § Notes — never an error. The MATERIA.md § Review angles and § Design tool prose, the review-angles README intro, and the status-template stage-review § Notes convention blocks are all hand-reconciled in files the user repo freely edits — no doctor check can honestly flag their absence.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold to match the current scaffold: the five new .materia/review-angles/{design-coherence,design-feasibility,design-fidelity,architecture-grounding,architecture-coverage}.md angle files (copy from the current scaffold — mission-only bodies); MATERIA.md § Review angles (the five new registry rows, the design-stage/architecture-stage Gate-token definitions, the canonical-count/self-description rewrite from seven to twelve, and the stage-review tier-default+override sentence); MATERIA.md § Design tool's Authoring budget bullet (the pre-gate stage-review touch pricing — up to 3 further authoring touches on top of the existing ≤4 gate-bound touches, each priced alike); .materia/review-angles/README.md's intro (now naming both review kinds — the post-implementation fan-out and the stage-review points); the stage-review § Notes convention blocks in .materia/docs/specs/_templates/status.md (both design-stage and architecture-stage) and .materia/docs/bugs/_templates/status.md (architecture-stage only — no design-stage point on the bug lane); the design template's Feedback-log comment (naming both revision channels — a design-gate revision round or a pre-gate stage-review revision round); and .materia/docs/specs/README.md + .materia/docs/bugs/README.md's pipeline-depiction updates (the stage-reviewed annotations on the design/architecture/bug-analysis rows and the review-fan-out row's split between its seven post-implementation angles and the five stage-review-only registry rows). The loops themselves — ship-spec § Stage reviews (design & architecture), fix-bug § Bug-analysis stage review, spawn-contract Block 3a, and the design/architecture/bug-analysis stage-skill reconciliation — live in the plugin skills and update with the plugin; a repo that skips this adoption simply has its stage reviews skip-and-record (the zero-rows path above), losing the capability but breaking nothing.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). No artifactSchema bump — angle-library membership and the hand-reconciled MATERIA.md/status-template prose sit outside the tracked installed-artifact contract (release/README.md). The `ledger` surface token is deliberately omitted: appending a Change entry is not a change to ledger structure/machinery (the 0.3.0 convention — sibling entries that merely append do not self-list `ledger`). Classified recommended (operator decision at intake): a repo that skips adoption loses the stage-review capability entirely — the loops skip-and-record at both points — while an untouched repo keeps working unaffected; a real capability loss, not a graceful degrade-to-default, the same posture as 0.3.0-design-conformance-angle.

changes.summary: Relocates the agent-facing docs tree from the repo-root docs/ to .materia/docs/ so the harness's docs can no longer be confused with (or collide with) a repo's own human-facing docs/; skills, doctor's design-gate scan, the check:docs gate + oracle, and the validator all move with it, and the installed-artifact schema bumps 3 -> 4.
changes.manualMigration: Relocate the agent-facing docs tree from docs/ to .materia/docs/ and stamp artifact schema 4: (1) git mv docs .materia/docs — the whole tree moves intact (specs, bugs, epics, research, standards, resources, _templates, the router and the top-level pages); (2) repair the moved tree's tree-escaping relative links, which now sit one directory deeper than they resolve — e.g. .materia/docs/README.md's ../MATERIA.md becomes ../../MATERIA.md and .materia/docs/standards/skills.md's ../../CLAUDE.md becomes ../../../CLAUDE.md; run the refreshed gate (sh .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh) to enumerate the rest and repair each path by hand (path-only mechanical repair is permitted inside frozen dated run folders — .materia/docs/specs/<slug>/, .materia/docs/bugs/<slug>/, and .materia/docs/bugs/_reports/<slug>/ — the freeze protects content, not byte-stasis against a harness move); (3) refresh .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh from the plugin scaffold — the installed gate scans the old docs/ roots and would otherwise lint your own human docs/; back up the old copy to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh.pre-schema4 first; (4) stamp artifactSchema 4 in .materia/project.json; (5) sweep the consumers that still name the old paths — the repo CLAUDE.md links, MATERIA.md literals, .materia/docs/standards/docs.md's enforcement-path list, the installed .materia/review-angles/ files' docs-path references, package scripts, CI workflows, and settings allowlists; the deep run-folder prefixes (docs/specs, docs/bugs, docs/epics, docs/research) are the mechanical ones, while human-collision-prone shallow names (docs/README, docs/standards, docs/product, docs/glossary) need judgement, and frozen dated run folders are exempt from this reference sweep (the link-path repair in step 2 is the one sanctioned touch inside them). If a partially-migrated docs/ tree remains after the move, remove it by hand once you have verified its contents moved. /materia:migrate --apply automates the move, the gate refresh, and the stamp; this by-hand path is for an un-migrated repo adopting without it.
changes.notes: Discoverability + detection: unlike the sibling 0.4.0 entries this change IS detectable — doctor's docs-location check reports drift (a legacy docs/README.md router present with .materia/docs/ absent) and, as a breaking change in the (3,4] schema window, escalates a not-yet-migrated repo to blocked; a docs-less repo (both trees absent) reads docs-location ok and is filtered as already-adopted, so it is never nagged. artifactSchema bumps 3 -> 4: the agent-docs tree's canonical location is part of the installed-artifact contract (the same reasoning that made the 0.3.0 gate-script relocation a schema move), so latest.json, the scaffold's .materia/project.json, and this file move to 4 in lockstep. Migratable: relocate-docs auto-moves the tree when the drift is unambiguous, refreshes the stale-roots gate script (backing the old bytes up to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh.pre-schema4, the one deliberate overwrite in migrate — it never destroys bytes), and stamps schema 4; a present hand-authored schema<2/unknown state is the project-state floor migrate refuses to touch, left to manualMigration. The move never rewrites file contents — the moved tree's now-short escaping links and other stale consumers are surfaced for by-hand repair, not auto-edited. The `ledger` surface token IS listed here (unlike the sibling appends): this entry lands alongside a real schema bump and the release machinery's four-way coherence moves with it. Classified breaking (operator decision at intake): after the plugin updates, skills / doctor / check-docs only look at .materia/docs/, so an un-migrated repo's old docs/ tree is no longer discovered — old artifacts are unsupported without migration, which ships in this same release. Because 0.4.0 is a pending, untagged release, a repo already installed on pending-0.4.0 sees no version-string change and must uninstall+reinstall to pick up this breaking relocation.

changes.summary: After finalize opens the PR, the orchestrator now watches it on every ship-spec/fix-bug run — polling CI, fixing failures (<=3 rounds), and resolving merge conflicts (merge-baseline, never rebase) — then takes a posture terminal: an interactive run surfaces the PR at green for the human to review + merge (a bounded stuck-pending watch prevents an indefinite session pin), while an --auto autopilot run additionally merges (the prior autopilot-only 'Merge watch' behavior, unchanged). The autopilot-only ship-spec section is generalized into the shared PR watch; finalize hands into it and fix-bug runs its notify terminal only (fix-bug has no --auto, so it never merges).
changes.detectionNotes: The change is behavior in the plugin skills (ship-spec/finalize/fix-bug), which auto-update with the plugin; an installed repo needs no artifact change to get the watch. The only installed-facing surface is hand-reconciled scaffold prose — the STATUS templates' pr-watch: convention comments, the specs/bugs READMEs, standards/skills.md, and the always-loaded scaffold CLAUDE.md — which doctor's inspect() never scans (it keys only on docs-location / check-docs-sh presence+location / project.json). A stale copy of that prose is indistinguishable from a deliberate local edit, so no doctor check can honestly flag it; the skill writes the pr-watch: Notes lines into per-run STATUS.md whether or not the installed template documents the convention.
changes.manualMigration: Optional prose refresh only — no repo action is needed to get the watch (it lives in the plugin skills and updates with the plugin). To keep the materialized scaffold docs accurate, update: .materia/docs/specs/_templates/status.md (generalize the 'Autopilot merge-watch convention' Notes block to both terminals and add the interactive pr-watch: notify vocabulary); .materia/docs/bugs/_templates/status.md (add the same pr-watch: notify convention — the bug lane is notify-only, no auto-merge); .materia/docs/specs/README.md and CLAUDE.md (the post-finalize flow now watches on every run — interactive surfaces at green, --auto merges — not autopilot-only); .materia/docs/standards/skills.md (the orchestrator always watches/fixes/resolves; --auto additionally merges). Rename any 'ship-spec Merge watch' reference to 'ship-spec PR watch'.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). No artifactSchema bump — the change is skill behavior plus hand-reconciled scaffold prose / per-run STATUS Notes vocabulary, all outside the tracked installed-artifact contract. The ledger surface token is omitted per the sibling-append convention (0.4.0-stage-reviews): a plain Change append is not a change to ledger structure/machinery. Classified optional (operator decision, flagged for veto): the watch behavior arrives with the plugin and an un-refreshed repo loses no capability and gets no wrong behavior — the same posture as 0.4.0-init-staged-intake; the scaffold prose refresh is documentation catch-up, not a functional adoption. fix-bug gets the notify terminal only — it has no --auto, so standards/skills.md's '--auto is a no-op in every other skill' stays true.

changes.summary: Adds '### Maintainer lifecycle — the shared contract' to standards/skills.md — one spine (preflight → sweep → classify → branch → fix → verify → pre-PR adversarial review rounds ≤3 → PR → ride CI ≤3) shared by all four maintainers; retrofits janitor and librarian onto it (oversized findings now file as queue entries carrying a registered source key, the librarian's queue-entry path restricted to proposed specs only, and any run that files a queue entry forfeiting the librarian's auto-merge, unifying with its existing scale-guard/MATERIA.md forfeits into one mechanism); and retires ui-inspection, replacing it with two live-app maintainers under a shared '#### UI maintainers — the live-app envelope' subsection — curator (visual-standards sweep) and concierge (experience sweep) — both restricted to a presentation-layer-only edit envelope and both verifying fixes via re-capture (before/after capture pairs riding the PR) alongside the full local gate.
changes.detectionNotes: standards/skills.md, MATERIA.md, the queue READMEs, and CLAUDE.md are hand-reconciled prose the installed repo freely edits — doctor's inspect() never scans them (it keys only on docs-location / check-docs-sh presence+location / project.json), so an un-refreshed installed copy is indistinguishable from a deliberate local edit, the same posture as every other 0.4.0 scaffold-prose entry. The removal case (a stale ui-inspection string) differs from the addition cases only in that it would be unambiguous if scanned — a leftover reference to a retired skill can't be a legitimate independent choice the way a missing new section can — but this release's consistent practice, matching its siblings, is that no doctor check exists for hand-reconciled scaffold-prose drift in either direction; only the tracked installed-artifact contract (docs-location, check-docs-sh, project.json shape) is machine-checked.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold to match the current one: (1) .materia/docs/standards/skills.md — add § Maintainer lifecycle (the ten-step spine, § Pre-PR review rounds, § Oversized findings, § UI maintainers — the live-app envelope) and drop the retired Producer-lifecycle Autonomous-checkpoint / Branch-at-discovery variants (ui-inspection was their sole user); update § Skill kinds' Maintainer row (janitor/librarian/curator/concierge, oversized-findings + review-rounds language) and Producer row (ui-inspection removed); update § Registration surfaces' producer-source-key trigger row so it also fires for a maintainer's queue-entry source key. (2) MATERIA.md — replace the ui-inspection mention in § Eyes with curator/concierge, reword the § Skill routing 'Coverage, not partition' accounting paragraph to enumerate all seven operator-session parents (janitor, librarian, curator, concierge, ship-spec, propose-epic, triage-retros); add janitor: reviewer, librarian: reviewer, curator: reviewer, concierge: reviewer routing rows; in the rowless Operator-session skills list, drop ui-inspection and add curator/concierge (each noting its <skill>: reviewer row), and reword librarian's and janitor's entries for their new reviewer rows. (3) the scaffold CLAUDE.md maintainer paragraph — /materia:janitor, /materia:librarian, /materia:curator, /materia:concierge fix bounded drift directly and file oversized findings as queue entries in the same PR. (4) both queue producer tables — .materia/docs/specs/_proposed/README.md and .materia/docs/bugs/_reports/README.md — reactivate the janitor row (no longer 'Legacy key — writes no new entries'), add a librarian row (proposed specs only) to _proposed/README.md, add curator/concierge rows to both, and retarget ui-inspection's row to a retired/Legacy-key row noting it as superseded. (5) .materia/docs/specs/README.md and .materia/docs/bugs/README.md — replace their ui-inspection producer mentions with the UI maintainers. Action item (not optional): /materia:ui-inspection no longer resolves once the plugin cache updates — re-point any scheduled/cron invocation of it at /materia:curator and /materia:concierge.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). Classified recommended, not breaking, for a vanishing operator-invocable command — flagged for explicit operator veto in the landing PR. Reasoning: no tracked installed artifact (release/README.md's installed-artifact contract: canonical file locations + .materia/project.json shape) becomes unsupported or needs migration — the skill itself is plugin-cache, materialized nowhere into the installed repo, so its removal sits outside that contract entirely; the replacement commands (curator, concierge) ship in the very same plugin update, so there is no window where the capability is simply gone; and existing queue entries carrying source: ui-inspection remain valid history the consumers (bugs/_reports/README.md et al.) still read without complaint — source keys are a registration convention, not an enforced enum, so a historical value never becomes invalid. breaking is reserved for old artifacts left unsupported without migration; a vanished command with an in-place replacement and no orphaned artifact reads differently, but the call is close enough that it is called out explicitly for the operator to veto. No artifactSchema bump: this is hand-reconciled prose plus plugin-skill behavior, both outside the tracked installed-artifact contract (release/README.md § Plugin semver is not the artifact schema). The ledger surface token is omitted per the sibling-append convention (0.4.0-stage-reviews): a plain Change append is not a change to ledger structure/machinery. The validator surface token is deliberately omitted even though scripts/validate-plugin.mjs changed (citer-list enrollments — curator/concierge into VC_CITERS/GATE_CITERS, ui-inspection removed — and the §2c UI self-gate map): those are repo-CI pins consumed only by this repo's own gate, never by an installed repo's doctor/migrate, the same posture 0.4.0-stage-reviews takes for its own pin changes (contrast 0.3.0-scripts-relocation, whose validator changes WERE doctor-consumed).

changes.summary: The Materia sigil's closing italic clause becomes 'base matter → gold, every run feeds the backlog' (the greppable anchor 'Forged with [Materia]' and the § PR attribution heading are unchanged); operator-facing flavor also lands in skill announcement strings outside the scaffold — init's interview welcome/handoff, doctor's human-summary framing (with an added verbatim-verdict guard), the four maintainers' zero-drift closing lines, and ship-spec/triage-retros' closing payoffs.
changes.detectionNotes: standards/skills.md § PR attribution is materialized once at init and thereafter user-owned prose the installed repo freely edits; an installed copy still carrying the old closing clause is indistinguishable from a deliberate local edit (the sigil line's shape, not its wording, is the only invariant doctor could check, and nothing checks even that). The flavor added to init/doctor/maintainer/ship-spec/triage-retros announcement strings lives entirely in the plugin skills, not in any materialized scaffold file, so it carries no detection surface at all — those skills simply behave differently once the plugin cache updates, with nothing for a repo to adopt or drift from.
changes.manualMigration: Optional: to have this repo's own forged PRs carry the new closing clause, update .materia/docs/standards/skills.md § PR attribution — the sigil's fenced example and its two explanatory bullets (the closing-clause split between the literal 'every run feeds the backlog' and the flavor-only 'base matter → gold', and the greppability parenthetical naming the anchor). An un-refreshed repo's PRs simply keep the prior closing clause (Forged with [Materia] ... cast by `<skill>` · every run feeds the backlog), which remains entirely valid — the anchor 'Forged with [Materia]' and the one-per-PR/always-last rules are unchanged either way.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). Classified optional: nothing consumes the sigil's closing-clause wording mechanically (it is attribution text, not a contract skills or tooling parse) and the prior line remains fully legal, so this is pure taste, not a capability gap. No artifactSchema bump — hand-reconciled prose outside the tracked installed-artifact contract. The ledger and validator surface tokens are omitted per the same conventions as this release's other appends (0.4.0-stage-reviews): a plain Change append touches neither ledger structure nor a doctor/migrate-consumed validator pin.

changes.summary: Two scaffold template corrections from the 0.4.0 final review pass: the spec retro template's first-level-subagent enumeration adds the missing ui-test-plan and reconcile-epic touchpoints (matching ship-spec/SKILL.md § Retrospective capture and the template's own Stage enum), and the bug STATUS template's § Notes gains the ui-coverage-waiver convention block the spec template already carries (finalize's e2e-coverage gate runs unchanged on UI-affecting bug fixes).
changes.detectionNotes: Both files are materialized-once, hand-reconciled template prose the installed repo freely edits — doctor's inspect() never scans the docs templates, and an un-refreshed copy is indistinguishable from a deliberate local edit, the same posture as this release's other scaffold-prose entries. Runtime behavior is unaffected either way: the retro template's Stage enum and ship-spec's § Touchpoints already carry the correct touchpoint list, and finalize's SKILL.md (the e2e-coverage gate step) spells out the ui-coverage-waiver line format it reads.
changes.manualMigration: Update the two materialized templates to match the current scaffold: in .materia/docs/specs/_templates/retro.md add ui-test-plan and reconcile-epic (when the epic gate ran it) to the first-level-subagent enumeration in the header comment; in .materia/docs/bugs/_templates/status.md add the ui-coverage-waiver § Notes convention block (copy from the current scaffold — the spec template's block, reworded for a UI-affecting fix). No repo action is needed for behavior: both conventions are read by the plugin skills, which update with the plugin.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). No artifactSchema bump — hand-reconciled template prose outside the tracked installed-artifact contract. The ledger surface token is omitted per the sibling-append convention (0.4.0-stage-reviews). Classified optional (conservative call, flagged for operator veto in the landing PR): both are documentation corrections whose authoritative content already lives in the plugin skills — an un-refreshed repo loses no capability (the waiver escape hatch works whether or not the installed template documents it), the same posture as 0.4.0-init-staged-intake.
